listing.title: F1 Singapore Grand Prix 2026 - Pit Grandstand
listing.description: Selling 2x F1 Singapore GP tickets. Pit Grandstand. Can only do PayNow transfer, meet-up not possible.

review_texts: Fast reply but item never arrived

event.name: Formula 1 Singapore Airlines Singapore Grand Prix 2026

matches.listing_title: F1 SG GP 2026 Pit Grandstand - CHEAP!!

verdict.reasoning:
EVIDENCE ANALYSIS:

1. PRICING ANOMALY (CRITICAL): Listed at S$150 — 78.5% below the official face value of S$698 and 74.1% below the market average of S$580. This is in the bottom 3rd percentile of 24 scanned listings. Extreme underpricing is the strongest individual scam indicator.

2. SELLER PROFILE (CRITICAL): Account is only 12 days old with 47 listings across unrelated categories (Tickets + Electronics). Only 2 reviews, one reporting non-delivery. This pattern matches known scam account behavior — new accounts flooding multiple categories.

3. EVENT VERIFICATION (CLEAR): The F1 Singapore Grand Prix 2026 is a verified real event at Marina Bay Street Circuit. Pit Grandstand tickets exist at S$698 face value. Event is NOT sold out, meaning legitimate resale at this discount is highly unlikely.

4. CROSS-PLATFORM DUPLICATES (WARNING): Same seller appears on Facebook Marketplace under slightly different name ('Fast Tickets SG' vs 'fasttickets_sg') with nearly identical listing (92% text similarity). Cross-platform posting with name variations is a common scam pattern.

5. LISTING AUTHENTICITY (WARNING): PayNow-only payment with no meet-up option removes all buyer protection. Legitimate sellers typically offer platform-mediated payment or in-person exchange.

CONCLUSION: Multiple converging signals — extreme underpricing, brand-new account, cross-platform duplicates, and payment red flags — produce high confidence that this is a fraudulent listing.
